Benjamin Ravenscroft
🏡 Home 📄 Research 📚 Teaching 👤 About

About

I am a first-year graduate student in the Operations, Information and Decisions Department at The Wharton School. My primary interests lie in empirical and data-driven research, focussed on healthcare operations.


I completed my undergraduate studies in 2023 at Queen’s University, majoring in Computer Science with a specialization in Biomedical Computing. As an undergrad, I worked in the Queen’s Cancer Research Institute as a research assistant, operating in both the Berman and Simpson Labs. My research primarily focussed on identifying predictive immune biomarkers for Non-Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer (NMIBC). During my undergraduate thesis, I worked on translating spatial-clustering algorithms developed for geoscience research for use in extracting interpretable features from multiplex immunofluorescence images of NMIBC that could be used as predictive biomarkers.


Under the supervision of Prof. Hossein Abouee-Mehrizi, I received my master’s degree in Management Science and Engineering from the University of Waterloo in 2025. While completing my master’s, I worked on data-driven research relating to the operations of a large pediatric outpatient rehabilitation network in Southwestern Ontario.

Education

PhD at The Wharton School, University of Pennsylvania

  • Operations, Information and Decisions Department (Operations Management Track)
  • Expected: 2030

M.ASc. at University of Waterloo

  • Management Science and Engineering
  • Advised by Prof. Hossein Abouee-Mehrizi (2023-2025)

B.Comp. at Queen's University

  • Bachelor's of Computing with a Specialization in Biomedical Computing (Honors)
  • Undergraduate thesis supervised by Prof. Amber Simpson
  • Undergraduate research supervised by Prof. David Berman M.D., PhD
